context.directive: Start from the live crate/workspace shape; prefer local crate patterns over generic Rust advice; keep ownership explicit; treat compiler errors as design feedback; validate narrowly first, then widen.
workflow: Observe rg --files, Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock, crate-local docs, and nearby tests.
Classify member vs standalone crate vs generated bridge before choosing commands.
Use typed Rust APIs and local module patterns over ad hoc string parsing.
Validate the smallest crate or manifest that owns the behavior.
Record reusable Rust patterns, bugs, validators, and postmortems as skill-pack signals.
anti_patterns: Assuming the repository has one top-level Cargo workspace.
Adding code that depends on an undeclared manifest edge.
Treating formatting or grep as runtime proof.
